summary: At exact HEAD 3da7bb249c8ac1212f53f26bf06432fb1d7d6071, One-command PR-ready Closure preserves the run-session architecture boundary. The independent-review action re-reads the selected Run immediately before execution, derives its repository from the validated managed-worktree authority, and carries current safe-action HEAD/journal state without losing freshly persisted runtime-dispatch provenance. No run-session module imports the CLI, and the final Story composes rather than duplicates the owner contracts merged by PR #372, #377, and #382. OCR-S-8 is staged and non-circular: PR creation, CI import, closure-state update, re-binding, and explicit execute merge remain post-acceptance delivery operations.
inspection_summary: Inspected the origin/main...HEAD source and documentation surface, current-head run-session/review/runtime topology, design SSOT child registration, parent roadmap staged completion state, exact Task target projection, runtime typed-stop normalization, safe-action human-stop compatibility and suffix containment, scope-boundary exact-path behavior, exact predecessor merge commits, current Story acceptance and Post-PR Delivery Closure Record, target architecture conformance, and exact-head unit, integration, E2E, typecheck, and targeted-validation artifacts. The final 3da7bb24 repair eliminates the stale lexical review root and stale implementation provenance concern by reloading current Run authority per review action while retaining the safe-action current HEAD.
inspection_inputs: codebase-memory import/call topology for guarded run-session and independent review
source import inspection for run-session owners importing ./cli.js
judgment_delta: Initial concern: buildIndependentReviewRunner could retain the Run snapshot and repository root captured before implementation or repair -> Final conclusion: the runner now calls loadSelectedRun immediately before each review action, derives the validated selected Run managed-worktree path, and passes the freshly persisted runtime dispatches while overlaying current safe-action HEAD and journal.

Initial concern: managed-worktree canonicalization could diverge between dispatch identity and review repository selection -> Final conclusion: loadSelectedRun validates the selected execution authority and git identity first, while review uses the persisted managed-worktree path required by runtime dispatch equality; record/poll/checkpoint callbacks reload the same selected Run authority.

Initial concern: the final Story might reimplement Autonomous Action DAG, Production Runtime Connectors, or Independent Review Orchestration -> Final conclusion: merge commits for PR #372, #377, and #382 are present and cited as predecessor SSOTs. The new closure module is callback-injected policy composition; connector changes propagate bounded objective data, and review changes invoke the existing review verdict/lifecycle owner rather than replace it.

Initial concern: one-command orchestration could introduce a run-session-to-CLI reverse dependency -> Final conclusion: no src run-session owner imports ./cli.js; src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js imports only timer support and receives bounded operations by callback. Exact-head conformance reports new_run_session_cli_reverse_dependency=false and improves total violations from origin/main 81 to 80.

Initial concern: OCR-S-8 might require evidence that can exist only after PR creation or merge, creating a circular Gate -> Final conclusion: pre-PR acceptance requires predecessor canonical evidence, non-duplication, and active staged Story/roadmap state only. PR creation, CI import, focused completion commit, current-HEAD rebind, re-review/re-CI, and explicit execute merge are tracked separately in the Post-PR Delivery Closure Record.

Initial concern: Design SSOT or parent-roadmap state could prematurely declare delivery closure -> Final conclusion: design-ssot.json keeps the roadmap active and registers the final ADR, test plan, and Story as required implementation/decomposition children; the roadmap also remains active with AIC-S-2/AIC-S-3 unchecked until post-PR delivery evidence closes all four Stories.

Initial concern: implementation delegation could receive broad or reference-only Task targets -> Final conclusion: src/task-manager.js preserves explicit target_files and only infers exact non-glob allowed paths from mutation-bearing mentions. test/scope-boundary-gate.test.js proves suffix lookalikes, English/Japanese negative constraints, and read-only references are excluded while explicit targets remain authoritative.

Initial concern: typed runtime/human stops or repair replay could escape bounded orchestration -> Final conclusion: test/agent-runtime-adapter.test.js verifies provider, missing-capability, recovery Run identity, review sandbox, and usage-accounting normalization; test/safe-action-orchestrator.test.js preserves legacy human-stop compatibility, requires structured autonomous decisions, and stops every dependent suffix on typed terminal states. The exact-head targeted-validation artifact includes all three suites.

Initial concern: current-head repair could lack regression proof across the full autonomous path -> Final conclusion: exact-head evidence passes 155 focused unit tests, 12 connector integration tests, 5 public-CLI E2E tests, typecheck, and 287 targeted validations. E2E observes implementation commit, independent needs_changes, repair commit, fresh review pass, and current-HEAD final prepare.
